title: Fundraiser

jokes.text: Smokey's dead. Smokey the Bear? Smokey Robinson, Pam. He died like an hour ago.
jokes.setup: Someone named 'Smokey' died, Pam assumes it's the famous bear character
jokes.punchline: Ryan corrects her it's Smokey Robinson the musician, delivered with condescension
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke that works because of character dynamics - Ryan's pretentiousness vs Pam's innocent mistake

jokes.text: Did you like that Tracks of My Tears is maybe the last true love song ever written?
jokes.setup: Ryan is being overly emotional about Smokey Robinson's 'death'
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously pretentious hyperbole about one song being the 'last true love song'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan character moment - his desperate need to appear sophisticated and deep about music

jokes.text: Well, I second that emotion.
jokes.setup: Ryan says he's completely devastated about Smokey Robinson
jokes.punchline: Jim references another Smokey Robinson song title as a pun response
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - Jim's wit shining through while staying in the Smokey Robinson theme

jokes.text: Tracks of My Tears and what else? What are some more? What's one more?
jokes.setup: Nellie asks what Smokey Robinson's big songs were
jokes.punchline: Ryan can only name one song repeatedly, exposing his fake fandom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as Ryan gets exposed for not actually knowing Smokey Robinson's catalog

jokes.text: I'm sorry that I'm not a fan of Jason Mraz or the Beatles.
jokes.setup: Ryan is defensive about being questioned on his music knowledge
jokes.punchline: He dismissively lumps Jason Mraz with The Beatles as if they're equivalent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ryan's hipster pretentiousness leads him to casually dismiss The Beatles

jokes.text: You don't like the Beatles?
jokes.setup: Ryan just dismissed The Beatles
jokes.punchline: Pam's shocked reaction emphasizes how absurd Ryan's statement was
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction that underscores the absurdity of Ryan's Beatles dismissal

jokes.text: It says here the Smokey Robinson dead thing is a hoax. It's on CNN as of two minute ago.
jokes.setup: Everyone has been mourning Smokey Robinson's death
jokes.punchline: It was fake news all along, deflating Ryan's entire emotional display
jokes.explanation: Good payoff that deflates the entire premise and exposes Ryan's performative grief

jokes.text: $250 is nothing to the world's biggest Smokey Robinson fan.
jokes.setup: Concert tickets cost $250
jokes.punchline: Pam sarcastically calls Ryan the world's biggest fan, knowing he only knows one song
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that continues to needle Ryan about his exposed fake fandom

jokes.text: How can they make the Smoke Man play with someone like that?
jokes.setup: Paul Anka is opening for Smokey Robinson
jokes.punchline: Ryan calls him 'the Smoke Man' like he's a close personal friend
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - Ryan's desperate attempt to sound like an insider with the nickname

jokes.text: Don't call me a clown, Pam. You're better than that.
jokes.setup: Pam mentions 'Tears of a Clown' (another Smokey song)
jokes.punchline: Ryan completely misunderstands and thinks she's calling him a clown personally
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Ryan's paranoia and lack of actual music knowledge

jokes.text: I feel like I'm being strangled, like I'm at some erotic asphyxiation sex club over on I-84. The Red Room, say, or Dominick's.
jokes.setup: Robert complains about wearing a tie
jokes.punchline: He compares it to very specific sex clubs he apparently knows by name and location
jokes.explanation: Peak Robert California - casually dropping inappropriate sexual references with disturbing specificity

jokes.text: Could be a knife, could be a gun. Could be a series of guns, all lined up to shoot parallel.
jokes.setup: Dwight speculating about what weapon Andy might have
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal weapons to an absurd multiple-gun contraption
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight escalation from reasonable to completely absurd with total seriousness

jokes.text: Everybody told me if I moved to America I'd be murdered.
jokes.setup: The office is panicking about Andy potentially being violent
jokes.punchline: Nellie's matter-of-fact delivery about America's murder reputation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment playing on Nellie's British outsider perspective on American violence

jokes.text: It's going to be super weird.
jokes.setup: Andy is going to the fundraiser where Robert will be after being fired
jokes.punchline: Jim's repeated insistence that it will be 'weird' with deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Jim's characteristic understatement - calling an obviously awkward situation just 'weird'

jokes.text: The warehouse isn't downstairs. Is it? Who knew, right?
jokes.setup: Jim suggests Robert ask the warehouse workers directly
jokes.punchline: Reveals Robert doesn't even know where the warehouse is in his own building
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Robert's disconnect from basic office operations

jokes.text: Tonight could be the night that Darryl and I go from casual work friends to actual good friends. The only thing standing in our way is the contempt he seems to feel for me.
jokes.setup: Jim talking about becoming better friends with Darryl
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions the major obstacle is Darryl's contempt for him
jokes.explanation: Great talking head moment with Jim's self-aware deadpan about a serious relationship obstacle

jokes.text: Did anyone order a blast from the past with a side order of sexy?
jokes.setup: Andy makes a dramatic entrance at the fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Terrible pickup line trying to be smooth and confident
jokes.explanation: Cringey line that works because it's so bad and perfectly fits Andy's desperate overconfidence

jokes.text: Because I want to shake your body.
jokes.setup: Robert offers to shake Andy's hand
jokes.punchline: Andy turns it into a crude sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that's more uncomfortable than funny, fits Andy's desperate state

jokes.text: Where do I look? It's been so long since I did one of these things.
jokes.setup: Andy is doing a talking head confessional
jokes.punchline: He's lost the ability to do basic talking heads after being fired
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about the documentary format and Andy's displacement from the office world

jokes.text: No one's raping this guy.
jokes.setup: Creed bids on jujitsu lessons
jokes.punchline: Casually announces his rape-proof status to the room
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed - completely inappropriate comment delivered with total casual confidence

jokes.text: So, Dwight doesn't understand silent auctions. I guess he's the stupid guy in the office. Because up until now we didn't have one.
jokes.setup: Dwight is confused about how silent auctions work
jokes.punchline: Jim's dry observation that they finally found their 'stupid guy'
jokes.explanation: Great talking head with Jim's characteristic dry wit about office dynamics

jokes.text: The hero lives in this dystopian future and he flies around in a spaceship that's shaped like a treble clef.
jokes.setup: Andy describes his rock opera to Angela
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous premise with a music note-shaped spaceship
jokes.explanation: Appropriately ridiculous concept that shows Andy's delusional creative ambitions

jokes.text: This guy's having a breakdown.
jokes.setup: Andy enthusiastically describes his rock opera
jokes.punchline: Angela's blunt assessment delivered deadpan to camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela reaction - cutting straight to the truth with no emotion

jokes.text: I'm more likely to pick up at night, say, after 9:00.
jokes.setup: Senator gives Oscar his phone number about dog charity work
jokes.punchline: Suggests calling late at night, implying something sexual
jokes.explanation: Sets up the gay subplot with subtle innuendo that could be innocent but probably isn't

jokes.text: This confirms three things. I'm right about the Senator, I still got it, and poor Angela.
jokes.setup: Oscar thinks the Senator hit on him
jokes.punchline: His three takeaways, ending with pity for Angela
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Oscar's self-satisfaction and the Angela sympathy

jokes.text: Although he's humanized at the end because he weeps uncontrollably and pees in his pants.
jokes.setup: Andy describes the villain Thomas Oregon in his rock opera
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous humanizing moment involving crying and pants-wetting
jokes.explanation: Absurd character detail that shows Andy's amateurish writing approach

jokes.text: We're flying so high we're cracking the sky Going to fly out of this dome My girlfriend and I
jokes.setup: Andy starts singing his rock opera
jokes.punchline: Terrible lyrics delivered with complete earnestness
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad songwriting that creates cringe humor through Andy's delusion

jokes.text: Hey, jabroni, show some class.
jokes.setup: Andy is being disruptive at the fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Robert uses the wrestling term 'jabroni' in a formal setting
jokes.explanation: Good Robert California moment - unexpectedly using wrestling slang at a fancy fundraiser

jokes.text: You're being a Thomas Oregon.
jokes.setup: Andy is being disruptive
jokes.punchline: Erin uses Andy's own rock opera villain as an insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Erin was listening and uses Andy's creation against him perfectly

jokes.text: And a high five. Let's do that again.
jokes.setup: Andy buys a table at the fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Demands a high five from the volunteer and makes them do it twice
jokes.explanation: Physical cringe comedy showing Andy's desperate need for validation

jokes.text: He wanted to rock more than just my vote.
jokes.setup: Oscar insists the Senator was hitting on him
jokes.punchline: Sexual pun using 'rock' in political and physical contexts
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that works on multiple levels, fits Oscar's character

jokes.text: Life is Downton Abbey.
jokes.setup: Jim says life isn't Downton Abbey
jokes.punchline: Kevin immediately contradicts him with complete certainty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - confidently contradicting someone with zero explanation

jokes.text: Like candy from a baby.
jokes.setup: Dwight wins a gym membership at auction
jokes.punchline: Uses the wrong idiom (should be 'taking candy from a baby')
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's confidence despite getting the saying wrong

jokes.text: Then suddenly out of nowhere the US Military bought the patent from me for $20 million.
jokes.setup: David talks about his failed 'Suck It' toy invention
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous military purchase of a children's toy for huge money
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with absurd twist - the military buying a toy vacuum shows how random success can be

jokes.text: What's another food that we like? Tacos.
jokes.setup: Kevin listing foods he wants instead of fancy appetizers
jokes.punchline: His simple, childlike way of categorizing food preferences
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin - simple food preferences expressed in his characteristic childlike manner

jokes.text: I've never eaten a taco. I'm not entirely sure what they are. As long as they're not slimy, and please, God, don't let them have eyes.
jokes.setup: Nellie offers to get tacos
jokes.punchline: Reveals complete ignorance about tacos with disturbing expectations
jokes.explanation: Good Nellie character moment showing her British unfamiliarity with basic American food

jokes.text: A gay man wouldn't leave the store wearing those shoes.
jokes.setup: Oscar defending that Jim's shoes prove he's not gay
jokes.punchline: As a gay man, Oscar delivers harsh judgment on Jim's fashion choices
jokes.explanation: Great Oscar moment using his identity to deliver a savage fashion critique

jokes.text: Hey, you bought me these shoes.
jokes.setup: Oscar insults Jim's shoes
jokes.punchline: Jim reveals Pam bought them, making the insult worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the shoe insult hit both Jim and Pam's taste

jokes.text: You know what I said? Yes. Not a joke. That that was not even a joke.
jokes.setup: Robert is giving a speech about loving dogs
jokes.punchline: He clarifies that his answer wasn't meant to be funny, which makes it funnier
jokes.explanation: Good Robert moment where his attempt to clarify makes the moment more absurd

jokes.text: I will take all of those dogs.
jokes.setup: Robert is talking about elderly dogs needing homes
jokes.punchline: Andy impulsively volunteers to take all 12 dogs
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Andy's manic behavior and the consequences to follow

jokes.text: Hello, everyone. I'm Andy Bernard and I am going to take that bitch home.
jokes.setup: Andy interrupts Robert's speech to address the crowd
jokes.punchline: Uses 'bitch' referring to a female dog but sounds inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where technically correct word choice sounds completely wrong in context

jokes.text: That is a female dog reference. This bitch understands loyalty. Sassy human reference.
jokes.setup: Andy realizes his first 'bitch' comment sounded wrong
jokes.punchline: He clarifies then immediately uses it again in a different way
jokes.explanation: Escalation that makes Andy's clarification worse by immediately reusing the problematic word

jokes.text: You can administer it orally, but he's going to puke it up. So other end's best.
jokes.setup: Volunteer explaining how to give medicine to a dog
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions anal administration as the preferred method
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about the realities of pet care that Andy didn't consider

jokes.text: Kenny's a therapy dog. He apparently thinks you're in some kind of emotional crisis.
jokes.setup: Andy mentions a dog is bonding with him
jokes.punchline: The dog is trained to detect emotional distress
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic moment where the therapy dog correctly identifies Andy's mental state

jokes.text: Stupid dog.
jokes.setup: Dog correctly identifies Andy is in emotional crisis
jokes.punchline: Andy calls the dog stupid for being accurate
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that shows Andy's denial about his obvious emotional state

jokes.text: I'd have to be a monster to root for that. A lonely, aging monster.
jokes.setup: Oscar claims he's not disappointed the Senator wasn't hitting on him
jokes.punchline: Describes himself as a lonely, aging monster
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating moment where Oscar reveals his true feelings through harsh self-assessment

jokes.text: Nellie trying to eat tacos visual gag
jokes.setup: Nellie got tacos despite never having eaten one
jokes.punchline: Extended visual sequence of her struggling to eat them properly
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to earlier setup about Nellie's taco inexperience

jokes.text: All I had to do was look up the prices, idiots. Suck it!
jokes.setup: Dwight has won every auction item
jokes.punchline: He reveals he cheated and celebrates with 'Suck it!'
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment showing his competitive nature and casual use of inappropriate phrase

jokes.text: Your donation is the largest we've ever received at over $34,000.
jokes.setup: Dwight thought he was gaming the system
jokes.punchline: He accidentally donated a huge amount of money
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal where Dwight's scheme backfires spectacularly with huge financial consequences

jokes.text: When did it become about the flower arrangements? And the white wine spritzers? Hmm? And all the dinner rolls?
jokes.setup: Dwight is giving a speech after his huge donation
jokes.punchline: He rants about mundane party details while people expect wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's inability to give an appropriate speech despite the situation

jokes.text: These tables tarted up like Victorian whores.
jokes.setup: Dwight continues his inappropriate speech
jokes.punchline: Compares elegant table settings to prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Peak inappropriate Dwight - historically specific insult that's completely wrong for the venue

jokes.text: Why does this always happen to me?
jokes.setup: Senator says goodbye to Oscar after the fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Oscar laments his pattern of romantic disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Oscar's romantic frustration and self-pity

jokes.text: Yeah, he should be. It's his first day without a muzzle.
jokes.setup: Andy says one of his dogs is smiling
jokes.punchline: Reveals the dog was previously muzzled for being dangerous
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about the reality of taking dangerous rescue animals

jokes.text: This is literally the worst thing that has ever happened to you.
jokes.setup: Andy is trying to convince everyone he's doing great
jokes.punchline: Jim cuts through the delusion with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - cutting through denial with characteristic blunt honesty

jokes.text: Sometimes I feel like everyone I work with is an idiot. And by sometimes, I mean all times. All the times. Every of the time.
jokes.setup: Kevin reflecting on his coworkers
jokes.punchline: Escalates to 'every of the time' showing his own grammar issues
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment where his critique of others' intelligence is undermined by his own grammatical error

jokes.text: Doesn't really poop. It's perfect. Nothing to pick up.
jokes.setup: Kevin describing his adopted dog Ruby
jokes.punchline: He thinks not pooping is a positive trait
jokes.explanation: Dark setup for the reveal that Ruby is dead, Kevin missing obvious warning signs

jokes.text: I have to prop her eyes open so she can see it.
jokes.setup: Kevin puts TV on for his dog
jokes.punchline: Has to manually open the dog's eyes
jokes.explanation: Disturbing detail that makes it obvious the dog is dead while Kevin remains oblivious

jokes.text: She smells horrible. It's unbelievable. But I don't want to put her in the bath. I'm afraid that she'll drown.
jokes.setup: Kevin continues describing his 'dog'
jokes.punchline: Dog smells terrible but he won't bathe her for fear of drowning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin obliviousness - all the signs point to death but he interprets them as living dog problems

jokes.text: Man, that stinks.
jokes.setup: Kevin has been describing his dead dog Ruby
jokes.punchline: Final understatement about the literal stench while camera shows the obviously dead dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending to the Kevin dead dog sequence - literal and figurative 'stinks' with visual payoff

tmdb_overview: Unemployed Andy crashes a fundraiser for the Senator and winds up adopting twelve disabled dogs. Dwight discovers that winning an auction means losing money, and Darryl teaches Nellie how to eat a taco.
